analysis.note: Formally also masculine genitive singular; here taken as neuter because it resumes a neuter item in the verse-context, but the antecedent is not fixed in this ablation run.
Bhāṣya reading with double -tt-: vyāvṛttiḥ; external sandhi before vowel gives vyāvṛttir.
Abstract noun in -tva, ‘arhatship’; the supplied bhāṣya reading has geminate -ttv-.
Accusative object of the absolutive āśritya; final -t is voiced before ā-.
Indeclinable absolutive of ā-√śri, ‘having depended on / relying on’.
Ātmanepada form used intransitively: ‘proceeds, arises, operates’.
Neuter nominative singular agreeing with vijñānam; formally identical with accusative. Final -m is printed as anusvāra before m-.

verification.detail: compound stem tadālamba as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Name-word in apposition to vijñānam with indeclinable nāma; printed in sandhi as part of manonāma.
Indeclinable ‘by name, called’, not here the inflected noun nāman.
Subject of pravartate, with the adjectives tadālambam and mananātmakam and the name-apposition manaḥ nāma. Final -m is printed as anusvāra before m-.
Neuter nominative singular agreeing with vijñānam; classified semantically as a possessive -ātmaka compound.

verification.detail: compound stem mananātmaka as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

justifications.decision: The first clause is read as a nominal sentence: ‘of it, the turning-away is in arhatship.’ The locative arhattve marks the state or condition in which the vyāvṛtti occurs.
options_considered: ‘turning away’ as a literal motion term
‘deliverance from arhatship’ by misreading the locative
arhat + tva as two independent words
justifications.chosen: vyāvṛttiḥ as ‘turning-away / cessation’, located in arhattva ‘arhatship’.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. vyāvṛtti: ‘turning away ... deliverance from, getting rid of’; the form arhattve is the regular locative singular of a neuter abstract in -tva.
evidence.translation: The lexicon supports ‘turning away’ or ‘getting rid of’; the locative form supports ‘in arhatship’ rather than an ablative ‘from an arhat’.

justifications.decision: tat is accusative singular neuter governed by the absolutive āśritya; pravartate is an intransitive present middle/kartari form of pra-√vṛt. The subject is supplied later by vijñānam.
options_considered: ‘having taken that as refuge, it proceeds’
justifications.chosen: tad āśritya pravartate = ‘depending on that, it proceeds/arises’.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. ā-√śri: ‘to adhere, rest on ... to depend on’; MW s.v. pra-√vṛt: ‘to come forth, issue, originate, arise ... take place’.
evidence.translation: The dictionary meanings support ‘relying/depending on’ for āśritya and ‘proceeds/arises’ for pravartate.

justifications.decision: Because tadālambam agrees with neuter vijñānam and pravartate is intransitive, it is better read adjectivally than as an independent accusative object. The compound is therefore treated as bahuvrīhi in sense.
justifications.chosen: tadālambam as a possessive compound: ‘having that as its support/object’.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. ālamba: ‘that on which one rests or leans, support, prop’.
evidence.translation: ālamba can denote a support; tadālambam may therefore mean ‘whose support is that’.

justifications.decision: nāma is read as the indeclinable ‘by name, called’, so manaḥ is the name in apposition to vijñānam, not a separate predicate ‘mind is name’.
justifications.chosen: manaḥ nāma vijñānam = ‘a consciousness called mind’.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. nāma: ‘ind. ... by name i.e. named, called’.
evidence.translation: The indeclinable nāma regularly marks a naming expression: ‘called X’.

justifications.decision: The compound qualifies vijñānam. The final member ātmaka, in compound, supports ‘having/consisting of the nature of’; manana supplies the nature, ‘thinking/reflection’.
options_considered: ‘made of homage’ using the rarer sense of manana

evidence.quote: MW s.v. manana: ‘thinking, reflection, meditation’; MW s.v. ātmaka: ‘having or consisting of the nature or character of (in comp.)’.
evidence.translation: Together the members support ‘of the nature of thinking’.

justifications.decision: Although neuter nominative and accusative singular are formally identical, an intransitive pravartate requires a nominative subject, and the surrounding neuter forms cohere as subject plus appositional/adjectival qualifiers.
options_considered: reading the -am forms as accusative objects
reading vijñānam as nominative subject with agreeing qualifiers
justifications.chosen: The neuter -am forms tadālambam, vijñānam, and mananātmakam are nominative singular in this syntax.

evidence.quote: Grammatical reasoning: pravartate is intransitive; vijñānam supplies the neuter singular subject, while tadālambam and mananātmakam agree with it.
evidence.translation: The syntax favors nominative agreement rather than a chain of accusative objects.

translation:
Of it, the turning-away (vyāvṛtti) is in arhatship (arhattva).
Depending on that, there proceeds
that consciousness (vijñāna) called mind (manas):
having that as support/object (ālamba),
whose nature is thinking (manana).
one_shot_delta: Without commentary, the antecedents of tasya and the two occurrences of tad cannot be securely identified; the translation therefore leaves ‘it/that’ explicit rather than supplying a doctrinal referent.
A commentary-blind reading may under- or over-technicalize vyāvṛtti, choosing either a merely spatial ‘turning away’ or a doctrinal ‘cessation’ without evidence.
The analyzer’s arhat_tve split could lead to the false impression of two words; grammar favors the abstract locative arhattve, ‘in arhatship’.
A blind translation could take tadālambam as an accusative noun ‘that support’ rather than as an adjective of vijñānam, ‘having that as support/object’.
The technical sense of ālamba as ‘object’ rather than the dictionary’s broader ‘support’ remains risky without commentary.
analyzer_disagreements: arhattve: ByT5 segmented arhat_tve and treated tve as a form of tva. I take the bhāṣya reading as one word, locative singular neuter of the abstract stem arhattva, ‘arhatship’.
tadālambam: ByT5 split tad + ālambam. I retain the members but analyze the printed unit as the compound stem tadālamba, a possessive adjective qualifying vijñānam.
mananātmakam: ByT5 split manana + ātmakam. I analyze the printed unit as the compound stem mananātmaka, qualifying vijñānam.
āśritya: ByT5 gives the lemma āśri. I record the bare verbal root √śri with prefix ā-, while still treating the form as an indeclinable absolutive.
tasya: ByT5 gives a genitive singular pronoun without fixing gender. I record neuter, while noting that the form is also masculine and that the antecedent is unresolved without commentary.
open_questions: The precise antecedent of tasya is not fixed without commentary or preceding context.
The precise antecedent of tad in tad āśritya and tadālambam is not fixed in this ablation run.
The doctrinal force of vyāvṛtti—literal turning-away, exclusion, cessation, or removal—cannot be decided from the dictionary alone.
Whether ālamba should be translated more technically as ‘object’ rather than ‘support’ cannot be settled without commentary.
